core.label: COBOL Fundamentals
core.description: Divisions, data types, and business-oriented programming.

topics.label: Environment Setup
topics.description: Install GnuCOBOL or use an IBM mainframe emulator. COBOL powers banking and government systems.

topics.label: Program Structure (4 Divisions)
topics.description: IDENTIFICATION, ENVIRONMENT, DATA, and PROCEDURE divisions. Every COBOL program has this structure.

topics.label: Data Types & PIC Clauses
topics.description: PICTURE clauses define data formats — PIC 9, PIC X, PIC S9(5)V99. COBOL's unique data declaration.

topics.label: MOVE, ADD, SUBTRACT, MULTIPLY, DIVIDE
topics.description: Data manipulation verbs. COBOL reads like English — MOVE A TO B, ADD X TO Y.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: IF/ELSE/END-IF, EVALUATE (switch), PERFORM (loop/call). PERFORM is COBOL's loop and call mechanism.

topics.label: File Handling
topics.description: READ, WRITE, OPEN, CLOSE for sequential and indexed files. COBOL excels at batch file processing.

topics.label: Tables (Arrays)
topics.description: OCCURS clause for arrays, SEARCH for lookups. Fixed-size tables with indexing.

topics.label: STRING & UNSTRING
topics.description: Concatenate and split strings. COBOL's string manipulation capabilities.
